metadata.name: amplitude__daily_performance
metadata.comment: Each record represents performance metrics for each distinct day and event type.

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: The assigned type of event. Example 'Add Friend'

number_new_users.name: number_new_users
number_new_users.comment: Count of distinct new visitors for that day

daily_unique_key.name: daily_unique_key
daily_unique_key.comment: Surrogate key generated from the `source_relation`, `event_day`, and `event_type`.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: amplitude__event_enhanced
metadata.comment: Each record represents event data, enhanced with event type data and unnested event, group, and user properties.

unique_event_id.name: unique_event_id
unique_event_id.comment: A unique key for each event, concatenating source_relation, event_id, device_id, and client_event_time

unique_session_id.name: unique_session_id
unique_session_id.comment: Unique key of each session, concatenating source_relation, user_id, and session_id

amplitude_user_id.name: amplitude_user_id
amplitude_user_id.comment: Unique key for each user, which is user_id by default. If user_id does not exist, use amplitude_id

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: The assigned type of event. Example 'Add Friend'

event_time.name: event_time
event_time.comment: Amplitude timestamp (UTC) which is the client_event_time adjusted by the difference between server_received_time and client_upload_time, specifically: event_time = client_event_time + (server_received_time - client_upload_time) 2015-08-10T12:00:00.000000 We use this timestamp to organize events on Amplitude charts. Note: If the difference between server_received_time and client_upload_time is less than 60 seconds, the event_time will not be adjusted and will equal the client_event_time

unique_event_type_id.name: unique_event_type_id
unique_event_type_id.comment: A unique identifier for each event type.

session_id.name: session_id
session_id.comment: The session start time in milliseconds since epoch. All events within the same session share the same session ID. By default, the setting in Amplitude for the session property is session ID. All events with the same session ID and the same user ID will be grouped into the same session. The session ID does not have to be unique across multiple users. Session IDs for events sent via the Amplitude SDKs are automatically generated and managed. However, for events sent via the HTTP API, Amplitude defaults to a session ID of -1. This means the event is excluded from all session metrics.

session_event_number.name: session_event_number
session_event_number.comment: The number of the event within the session, ordered by time of event.

user_event_number.name: user_event_number
user_event_number.comment: A sequential count of events triggered by a user, indicating the order of events within a user’s activity.

group_types.name: group_types
group_types.comment: Group types. "Groups" in the source table, renamed here. See the Accounts documentation for more information.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: A readable ID you define for known users. While optional, it is recommended that you assign a user_id after signup or login as it allows Amplitude to merge cross-device activity and link prior anonymous events. IDs should not be set for anonymous users, and cannot be changed once set.

user_creation_time.name: user_creation_time
user_creation_time.comment: Event_time (UTC) of the user's first event

amplitude_id.name: amplitude_id
amplitude_id.comment: An internal ID used to count unique users.

app.name: app
app.comment: Project ID found in your project's Settings page

client_event_time.name: client_event_time
client_event_time.comment: Local timestamp (UTC) of when the device logged the event.

client_upload_time.name: client_upload_time
client_upload_time.comment: The local timestamp (UTC) of when the device uploaded the event.

server_received_time.name: server_received_time
server_received_time.comment: Amplitude timestamp (UTC) of when servers received the event

server_upload_time.name: server_upload_time
server_upload_time.comment: Amplitude timestamp (UTC) of when the event data was uploaded to Amplitude servers

data.name: data
data.comment: Dictionary where certain fields such as first_event and merged_amplitude_id are stored.

language.name: language
language.comment: The language set by the user

start_version.name: start_version
start_version.comment: App version the user was first tracked on

totals.name: totals
totals.comment: The total number of times the event has happened this week

value.name: value
value.comment: Name of the event in the raw data.

events_per_session.name: events_per_session
events_per_session.comment: The count of events in a session

session_started_at.name: session_started_at
session_started_at.comment: The time of the first event in a session

session_ended_at.name: session_ended_at
session_ended_at.comment: The time of the last event in a session

user_session_number.name: user_session_number
user_session_number.comment: A sequential identifier for each session initiated by a user, helping to track users across sessions.

session_started_at_day.name: session_started_at_day
session_started_at_day.comment: The date on which a session started.

session_ended_at_day.name: session_ended_at_day
session_ended_at_day.comment: The date on which a session ended.

session_length_in_minutes.name: session_length_in_minutes
session_length_in_minutes.comment: The length of the session, in minutes

is_first_user_session.name: is_first_user_session
is_first_user_session.comment: Boolean of whether or not a session is the user's first session

minutes_in_between_sessions.name: minutes_in_between_sessions
minutes_in_between_sessions.comment: The minutes in between sessions of a user.

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Surrogate key generated from the `source_relation`, `unique_event_id`, and `unique_event_type_id`.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: amplitude__sessions
metadata.comment: Each record represents a distinct session with aggregated metrics for that session.

unique_session_id.name: unique_session_id
unique_session_id.comment: Unique key of each session, concatenating source_relation, user_id, and session_id

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: A readable ID you define for known users. While optional, it is recommended that you assign a user_id after signup or login as it allows Amplitude to merge cross-device activity and link prior anonymous events. IDs should not be set for anonymous users, and cannot be changed once set.

events_per_session.name: events_per_session
events_per_session.comment: The count of events in a session

session_started_at.name: session_started_at
session_started_at.comment: The time of the first event in a session

session_ended_at.name: session_ended_at
session_ended_at.comment: The time of the last event in a session

session_length_in_minutes.name: session_length_in_minutes
session_length_in_minutes.comment: The length of the session, in minutes

user_session_number.name: user_session_number
user_session_number.comment: The number of the session, within a user's lifetime

last_session_ended_at.name: last_session_ended_at
last_session_ended_at.comment: The time the prior session ended at

last_session_ended_at_day.name: last_session_ended_at_day
last_session_ended_at_day.comment: The day the prior session ended

is_first_user_session.name: is_first_user_session
is_first_user_session.comment: Boolean of whether or not a session is the user's first session

minutes_in_between_sessions.name: minutes_in_between_sessions
minutes_in_between_sessions.comment: The minutes in between sessions of a user

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: amplitude__user_enhanced
metadata.comment: Each record represents a distinct user with aggregated metrics for that user.

amplitude_user_id.name: amplitude_user_id
amplitude_user_id.comment: Unique key for each user, which is user_id by default. If user_id does not exist, use amplitude_id

user_created_at.name: user_created_at
user_created_at.comment: Event_time (UTC) of the user's first event

first_session_at.name: first_session_at
first_session_at.comment: Timestamp of user's first recorded activity

last_session_at.name: last_session_at
last_session_at.comment: Timestamp of user's most recent activity

total_events_per_user.name: total_events_per_user
total_events_per_user.comment: The total number of events for a certain user

total_sessions_per_user.name: total_sessions_per_user
total_sessions_per_user.comment: The total number of sessions for a certain user

average_session_length_in_minutes.name: average_session_length_in_minutes
average_session_length_in_minutes.comment: Average length of sessions, in minutes

average_minutes_in_between_sessions.name: average_minutes_in_between_sessions
average_minutes_in_between_sessions.comment: Average time in between sessions for a user, in minutes

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_amplitude__event
metadata.comment: Table of all events tracked by Amplitude. Definitions are sourced from the Amplitude documentation [here](https://www.docs.developers.amplitude.com/analytics/apis/http-v2-api/#upload-request-body-parameters).

event_time.name: event_time
event_time.comment: Amplitude timestamp (UTC) which is the client_event_time adjusted by the difference between server_received_time and client_upload_time, specifically: event_time = client_event_time + (server_received_time - client_upload_time) 2015-08-10T12:00:00.000000 We use this timestamp to organize events on Amplitude charts. Note: If the difference between server_received_time and client_upload_time is less than 60 seconds, the event_time will not be adjusted and will equal the client_event_time.

event_day.name: event_day
event_day.comment: The day of the event time, in date format.

unique_session_id.name: unique_session_id
unique_session_id.comment: A unique key for each session, concatenating source_relation, user_id, and session_id.

amplitude_user_id.name: amplitude_user_id
amplitude_user_id.comment: Unique key for each user, which is user_id by default. If user_id does not exist, the package will use amplitude_id.

event_properties.name: event_properties
event_properties.comment: Event properties are attributes of the events your users fire, and which you then send to Amplitude. Each event will have its own set of event properties. The nature of these properties depends on both the type of product you have, and the specific information you're most interested in discovering. For instance, if Swipe is an event you're tracking, the event property Direction could have the values Left or Right. Some example event properties are description, category, type, duration, level, percent completed, count, source, status, number, lives, authenticated, error number, rank, action, and mode.

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: The assigned type of event. Example 'Add Friend'

group_types.name: group_types
group_types.comment: Group types. "Groups" in the source table, renamed here. See the [Accounts documentation](https://help.amplitude.com/hc/en-us/articles/115001765532-Accounts) for more information.

group_properties.name: group_properties
group_properties.comment: A dictionary of key-value pairs that represent data tied to the group. Each distinct value appears as a group segment on the Amplitude dashboard. You can store property values in an array, and date values are transformed into string values.

session_id.name: session_id
session_id.comment: The session start time in milliseconds since epoch.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: A readable ID you define for known users. While optional, it is recommended that you assign a user_id after signup or login as it allows Amplitude to merge cross-device activity and link prior anonymous events. IDs should not be set for anonymous users, and cannot be changed once set.

user_properties.name: user_properties
user_properties.comment: An attribute that describes a useful detail about the user it's attached to. Amplitude sends user properties with every event.

amplitude_id.name: amplitude_id
amplitude_id.comment: An internal ID used to identify unique users.

_insert_id.name: _insert_id
_insert_id.comment: A unique identifier for the event. Amplitude deduplicates subsequent events sent with the same device_id and insert_id within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device_id, user_id, event_type, event_id, and time.

ad_id.name: ad_id
ad_id.comment: (Android) Google Play Services advertising ID (AdID). This usually is wiped after ingestion and therefore will be blank.

app.name: app
app.comment: Project ID found in your project's Settings page.

client_event_time.name: client_event_time
client_event_time.comment: Local timestamp (UTC) of when the device logged the event.

client_upload_time.name: client_upload_time
client_upload_time.comment: The local timestamp (UTC) of when the device uploaded the event.

city.name: city
city.comment: City where the event was registered.

data.name: data
data.comment: Dictionary where certain fields such as first_event and merged_amplitude_id are stored. Ex, {"first_event":true}.

device_brand.name: device_brand
device_brand.comment: Brand of device where event was registered on.

device_carrier.name: device_carrier
device_carrier.comment: Carrier of device where event was registered on.

device_manufacturer.name: device_manufacturer
device_manufacturer.comment: Manufacturer of device where event was registered on.

device_model.name: device_model
device_model.comment: The model of device where event was registered on.

device_type.name: device_type
device_type.comment: Type of device where event was registered on.

idfa.name: idfa
idfa.comment: (iOS) Identifier for Advertiser. This usually is wiped after ingestion and therefore will be blank.

ip_address.name: ip_address
ip_address.comment: The IP address of the device which triggered the event.

language.name: language
language.comment: The language set by the user.

os_name.name: os_name
os_name.comment: OS name where event was registered on.

os_version.name: os_version
os_version.comment: OS version where event was registered on.

is_attribution_event.name: is_attribution_event
is_attribution_event.comment: Boolean of boolean of whether event was an attribution event.

library.name: library
library.comment: Library being used to send the event data.

is_paying.name: is_paying
is_paying.comment: Boolean of boolean of whether the user has ever logged any revenue, otherwise '(none)'. Note the property value can be modified via the\_Identify API.

platform.name: platform
platform.comment: Platform of the device where event was registered on.

processed_time.name: processed_time
processed_time.comment: Time of when event was processed.

region.name: region
region.comment: Region where event was registered on.

server_received_time.name: server_received_time
server_received_time.comment: Amplitude timestamp (UTC) of when servers received the event.

server_upload_time.name: server_upload_time
server_upload_time.comment: Amplitude timestamp (UTC) of when the event data was uploaded to Amplitude servers.

start_version.name: start_version
start_version.comment: App version the user was first tracked on.

user_creation_time.name: user_creation_time
user_creation_time.comment: Event_time (UTC) of the user's first event.

uuid.name: uuid
uuid.comment: A unique identifier per row (event sent).

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran synced a record.

unique_event_id.name: unique_event_id
unique_event_id.comment: A unique key for each event, concatenating source_relation, event_id, device_id, client_event_time, and amplitude_user_id.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_amplitude__event_type
metadata.comment: Table of all events types. Definitions are sourced from the Amplitude documentation [here](https://www.docs.developers.amplitude.com/analytics/apis/dashboard-rest-api/?h=flow_hidden).

display.name: display
display.comment: The display name of the event.

totals.name: totals
totals.comment: The total number of times the event has happened this week.

totals_delta.name: totals_delta
totals_delta.comment: Represents the change in event volume from the previous week.

value.name: value
value.comment: Name of the event in the raw data.

is_flow_hidden.name: is_flow_hidden
is_flow_hidden.comment: Boolean of whether the event is hidden from Pathfinder/Pathfinder Users or not.

is_hidden.name: is_hidden
is_hidden.comment: Boolean of whether the event is hidden or not.

is_in_waitroom.name: is_in_waitroom
is_in_waitroom.comment: Boolean of whether event type is in waitroom.

is_non_active.name: is_non_active
is_non_active.comment: Boolean of whether the event is marked inactive or not.

is_autohidden.name: is_autohidden
is_autohidden.comment: Boolean of whether event type is hidden.

is_deleted.name: is_deleted
is_deleted.comment: Boolean of whether event type is deleted.

is_timeline_hidden.name: is_timeline_hidden
is_timeline_hidden.comment: Boolean of whether the event is hidden or not.

is_waitroom_approved.name: is_waitroom_approved
is_waitroom_approved.comment: Boolean of whether is waitroom approved.

_fivetran_deleted.name: _fivetran_deleted
_fivetran_deleted.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran marked a record as deleted.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran synced a record.

unique_event_type_id.name: unique_event_type_id
unique_event_type_id.comment: A unique key for each event, concatenating source_relation, event_type_id, and project_name.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table
